# Coinbase.Net Full LLM Context

> Detailed AI/LLM reference for generating correct C#/.NET code with Coinbase.Net. Use this file when `llms.txt` is too brief and the task needs concrete examples, endpoint routing guidance, Coinbase Advanced Trade credential handling, symbol guidance, WebSocket routing, or anti-hallucination rules.

Coinbase.Net is a strongly typed .NET client library for Coinbase Advanced Trade REST/WebSocket APIs, selected Coinbase App REST endpoints, and Coinbase Exchange market-data APIs. It is part of the CryptoExchange.Net ecosystem and uses the same result, shared API, logging, rate limiting, dependency injection, and socket subscription patterns as the other JKorf exchange libraries.

Current package version in this repository: 3.10.1.
NuGet package id: JKorf.Coinbase.Net.
CryptoExchange.Net dependency: 11.1.0.
Target frameworks: netstandard2.0, netstandard2.1, net8.0, net9.0, net10.0.
Native AOT: supported on compatible .NET targets.

Important private-endpoint note: due to framework restrictions for signing requests, private endpoints currently require netstandard2.1 or a modern .NET target. Public endpoints are available on all supported target frameworks.

Primary documentation:
- README.md: overview, installation, feature table, release notes, basic examples
- docs site: https://cryptoexchange.jkorf.dev/Coinbase.Net/
- Coinbase Advanced Trade docs: https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/advanced-trade/docs/welcome
- Coinbase Exchange docs: https://docs.cdp.coinbase.com/exchange/docs/welcome
- API quick map: `docs/ai-api-map.md`
- source interfaces: `Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`
- AI examples: `Examples/ai-friendly/`

## Golden Rules For AI Code Generation

- Use `CoinbaseRestClient` for REST and `CoinbaseSocketClient` for WebSocket. Do not generate raw `HttpClient` calls to Coinbase endpoints.
- Use `restClient.AdvancedTradeApi` for modern Coinbase Advanced Trade market data, accounts, portfolios, trading, futures, perpetuals, and selected Coinbase App endpoints.
- Use `restClient.ExchangeApi` only for Coinbase Exchange API market-data endpoints exposed by this library.
- Use `socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi` for Advanced Trade WebSocket streams.
- Use `socketClient.ExchangeApi` for Coinbase Exchange WebSocket streams.
- Do not generate Binance/Bybit-style roots such as `SpotApi`, `UsdFuturesApi`, `CoinFuturesApi`, `GeneralApi`, or `V5Api`.
- Use `CoinbaseCredentials`, not generic `ApiCredentials`, for API key setup.
- Include `using Coinbase.Net;` when using `CoinbaseCredentials` or `CoinbaseEnvironment`.
- Coinbase Advanced Trade credentials are ECDSA key name/private key credentials. Do not generate HMAC secret/passphrase examples.
- Preserve PEM newlines when loading the private key from environment variables or secret stores.
- Always check `.Success` before reading `.Data`.
- REST methods return `HttpResult<T>` or `HttpResult`.
- WebSocket subscriptions and socket API requests return `WebSocketResult<T>` or `WebSocketResult`.
- Shared non-I/O symbol/cache helpers can return `ExchangeCallResult<T>`.
- Order placement has two success layers: check the `HttpResult` and then `CoinbaseOrderResult.Success`.
- Reuse clients. Do not instantiate a REST or socket client per request in production code.
- Prefer dependency injection for long-running services.
- Store WebSocket subscriptions and unsubscribe during shutdown.
- Do not call `.Result` or `.Wait()` on async methods.
- Do not invent endpoint names. If unsure, inspect `Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/**`.
- Use Coinbase dash-separated product ids such as `ETH-USD`, `BTC-USD`, and `ETH-USDT`.
- Do not use compact symbols such as `ETHUSDT`, underscore symbols such as `ETH_USDT`, slash symbols such as `ETH/USD`, or Bitfinex symbols such as `tETHUSD`.
- Let the library generate `clientOrderId` unless the user explicitly needs an idempotency/correlation id.
- For multi-exchange code, use `CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis` interfaces from `AdvancedTradeApi.SharedClient` and call `.Discover()` to inspect supported shared features.

## Installation

```bash
dotnet add package Coinbase.Net
```

The repository package id is `JKorf.Coinbase.Net`, but normal user install guidance in the README uses `dotnet add package Coinbase.Net`.

## Required Usings

Typical REST/trading code:

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net;
using Coinbase.Net.Clients;
using Coinbase.Net.Enums;
```

Shared API code:

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net.Clients;
using CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis;
```

Retry/error helper code may also need:

```csharp
using CryptoExchange.Net.Objects;
using CryptoExchange.Net.Objects.Sockets;
```

Dependency injection setup:

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net;
using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;
```

Interface injection:

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net.Interfaces.Clients;
```

## Client Setup

Public market data does not require credentials:

```csharp
var client = new CoinbaseRestClient();

var product = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH-USD");
if (!product.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(product.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(product.Data.LastPrice);
```

Private account and trading endpoints require Coinbase ECDSA credentials:

```csharp
var client = new CoinbaseRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials(
        "organizations/{org_id}/apiKeys/{key_id}",
        "-----BEGIN EC PRIVATE KEY-----\n...\n-----END EC PRIVATE KEY-----");
});
```

Socket client with credentials:

```csharp
var socketClient = new CoinbaseSocketClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials("API_KEY_NAME", "EC_PRIVATE_KEY");
});
```

When reading the private key from an environment variable, normalize escaped newlines:

```csharp
var privateKey = Environment
    .GetEnvironmentVariable("COINBASE_API_PRIVATE_KEY")
    ?.Replace("\\n", "\n");
```

Do not add a passphrase parameter; `CoinbaseCredentials` accepts key name and ECDSA private key.

## Environments And Options

Default REST environment is `CoinbaseEnvironment.Live`. `CoinbaseEnvironment.Live` is the only built-in environment in this repository.

```csharp
var live = new CoinbaseRestClient(options => options.Environment = CoinbaseEnvironment.Live);
```

Create custom endpoints only when explicitly requested:

```csharp
var custom = CoinbaseEnvironment.CreateCustom(
    "custom",
    restAddress: "https://api.coinbase.com",
    exchangeRestAddress: "https://api.exchange.coinbase.com",
    socketStreamPublicAddress: "wss://advanced-trade-ws.coinbase.com",
    socketStreamPrivateAddress: "wss://advanced-trade-ws-user.coinbase.com",
    socketClientPublicExchangeApiAddress: "wss://ws-feed.exchange.coinbase.com");
```

Common REST options:

```csharp
var client = new CoinbaseRestClient(options =>
{
    options.Environment = CoinbaseEnvironment.Live;
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials("API_KEY_NAME", "EC_PRIVATE_KEY");
    options.AutoTimestamp = true;
});
```

Common socket options:

```csharp
var socketClient = new CoinbaseSocketClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials("API_KEY_NAME", "EC_PRIVATE_KEY");
    options.SocketSubscriptionsCombineTarget = 10;
    options.ReceiveBufferSize = 524288;
});
```

`CoinbaseSocketOptions.SymbolsUsdcReplaceFilter` controls symbols for which USDC should not be replaced with USD when subscribing to streams. Most generated code should leave it unchanged.

## Dependency Injection

Current DI shape:

```csharp
services.AddCoinbase(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials("API_KEY_NAME", "EC_PRIVATE_KEY");
});
```

Inject:

```csharp
public class MyService
{
    private readonly ICoinbaseRestClient _restClient;
    private readonly ICoinbaseSocketClient _socketClient;

    public MyService(ICoinbaseRestClient restClient, ICoinbaseSocketClient socketClient)
    {
        _restClient = restClient;
        _socketClient = socketClient;
    }
}
```

Configuration binding is supported through `services.AddCoinbase(configuration)`. The DI extension registers shared REST and socket interfaces from `AdvancedTradeApi.SharedClient`.

## Result Handling Pattern

Always branch on `.Success`.

```csharp
var result = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("ETH-USD", limit: 50);
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"Code: {result.Error?.Code}");
    Console.WriteLine($"Message: {result.Error?.Message}");
    return;
}

var bestBid = result.Data.Bids.FirstOrDefault();
Console.WriteLine(bestBid?.Price);
```

For order placement, also check Coinbase's order response success:

```csharp
var order = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    "ETH-USD",
    OrderSide.Buy,
    NewOrderType.Limit,
    quantity: 0.01m,
    price: 2000m);

if (!order.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Error);
    return;
}

if (!order.Data.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Data.ErrorResponse.Message);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(order.Data.SuccessResponse.OrderId);
```

For retry logic, only retry transient errors:

```csharp
async Task<HttpResult<T>> WithRetry<T>(Func<Task<HttpResult<T>>> call, int maxAttempts = 3)
{
    HttpResult<T> last = default!;

    for (var attempt = 1; attempt <= maxAttempts; attempt++)
    {
        last = await call();
        if (last.Success)
            return last;

        if (last.Error?.IsTransient != true)
            return last;

        await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(250 * Math.Pow(2, attempt)));
    }

    return last;
}
```

Coinbase.Net returns normal API, validation, signing, and rate-limit failures through `.Error`; it generally does not throw for exchange rejections. Exceptions are for misconfiguration, disposal, cancellation, or programming errors.

## Main API Surface

REST:

```text
CoinbaseRestClient
  .AdvancedTradeApi
    .Account
    .ExchangeData
    .Trading
    .SharedClient
  .ExchangeApi
    .ExchangeData
```

WebSocket:

```text
CoinbaseSocketClient
  .AdvancedTradeApi
    .SharedClient
  .ExchangeApi
```

Important: Coinbase.Net does not use Binance or Bybit roots. Do not generate:

```text
restClient.SpotApi
restClient.UsdFuturesApi
restClient.CoinFuturesApi
restClient.GeneralApi
restClient.V5Api
socketClient.SpotApi
socketClient.V5SpotApi
```

## Symbol Routing

Coinbase uses dash-separated product ids:

```text
BTC-USD
ETH-USD
ETH-USDT
SOL-USD
```

Use `AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync(...)` to discover supported products and product types. Use `SymbolType.Spot` for spot products and `SymbolType.Futures` for futures products.

Examples:

```csharp
await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH-USD");
await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync(SymbolType.Spot);
await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetKlinesAsync("ETH-USD", KlineInterval.OneMinute);
await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("ETH-USD", 50);
```

Do not "normalize" Coinbase symbols to compact format for native Coinbase.Net methods.

## Intent To Method Map

For a broader quick reference, see `docs/ai-api-map.md`.

Common Advanced Trade market data tasks:

```text
Get server time -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetServerTimeAsync()
Get products -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync(...)
Get product -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH-USD")
Get order book -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("ETH-USD", limit)
Get candles -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetKlinesAsync("ETH-USD", KlineInterval.OneMinute, ...)
Get public trades -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetTradeHistoryAsync("ETH-USD", ...)
Get book ticker -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetBookTickerAsync("ETH-USD")
Get multiple book tickers -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetBookTickersAsync(...)
Get fiat assets -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetFiatAssetsAsync()
Get crypto assets -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetCryptoAssetsAsync()
Get exchange rates -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetExchangeRatesAsync(asset)
Get buy prices -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetBuyPriceAsync(asset)
Get sell prices -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSellPriceAsync(asset)
Get spot prices -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSpotPriceAsync(asset, date)
```

Common account and Coinbase App tasks:

```text
Get accounts -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetAccountsAsync(...)
Get account -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetAccountAsync(accountId)
Get portfolios -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetPortfoliosAsync(...)
Get portfolio breakdown -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetPortfolioAsync(portfolioId, asset)
Create portfolio -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.CreatePortfolioAsync(name)
Edit portfolio -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.EditPortfolioAsync(portfolioId, newName)
Delete portfolio -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.DeletePortfolioAsync(portfolioId)
Transfer portfolio funds -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.TransferPortfolioFundsAsync(...)
Get fee info -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetFeeInfoAsync(...)
Get API key info -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetApiKeyInfoAsync()
Get payment methods -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetPaymentMethodsAsync()
Get payment method -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetPaymentMethodAsync(paymentMethodId)
Create convert quote -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.CreateConvertQuoteAsync(...)
Get convert trade -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetConvertTradeAsync(...)
Commit convert trade -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.CommitConvertTradeAsync(...)
Get withdrawals -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetWithdrawalsAsync(accountId, ...)
Get withdrawal -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetWithdrawalAsync(accountId, withdrawalId)
Withdraw fiat -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.WithdrawAsync(...)
Deposit fiat -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.DepositAsync(...)
Get deposits -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetDepositsAsync(accountId, ...)
Get deposit -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetDepositAsync(accountId, depositId)
Get transactions -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetTransactionsAsync(accountId, ...)
Get transaction -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetTransactionAsync(accountId, transactionId)
Withdraw crypto -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.WithdrawCryptoAsync(...)
Create deposit address -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.CreateDepositAddressAsync(accountId, name)
Get deposit addresses -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetDepositAddressesAsync(accountId, ...)
Get deposit address -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetDepositAddressAsync(accountId, addressId)
```

Common futures/perpetual account tasks:

```text
Get perpetual summary -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetPerpetualPortfolioSummaryAsync(portfolioId)
Get perpetual balances -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetPerpetualBalancesAsync(portfolioId)
Set perpetual multi-asset collateral -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.SetPerpetualMultiAssetCollateralModeAsync(...)
Get futures balance summary -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetFuturesBalanceSummaryAsync()
Get futures intraday margin setting -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetFuturesIntradayMarginSettingAsync()
Set futures intraday margin setting -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.SetFuturesIntradayMarginSettingAsync(...)
Get futures current margin window -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetFuturesCurrentMarginWindowAsync(...)
```

Common trading tasks:

```text
Place order -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(symbol, side, newOrderType, ...)
Cancel order -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.CancelOrderAsync(orderId)
Cancel orders -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.CancelOrdersAsync(orderIds)
Edit order -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.EditOrderAsync(orderId, price, quantity)
Get order -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.GetOrderAsync(orderId)
Get orders -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.GetOrdersAsync(...)
Get fills/user trades -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.GetUserTradesAsync(...)
Close position -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.ClosePositionAsync(symbol, ...)
Get futures positions -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.GetFuturesPositionsAsync()
Get futures position -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.GetFuturesPositionAsync(symbol)
Get perpetual positions -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.GetPerpetualPositionsAsync(portfolioId)
Get perpetual position -> client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.GetPerpetualPositionAsync(portfolioId, symbol)
```

Coinbase Exchange API tasks:

```text
Get Exchange API server time -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetServerTimeAsync()
Get Exchange currencies -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetAssetsAsync()
Get Exchange products -> client.ExchangeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolsAsync()
```

Common WebSocket tasks:

```text
Advanced Trade heartbeat -> socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToHeartbeatUpdatesAsync(handler)
Advanced Trade trades -> socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToTradeUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Advanced Trade candles -> socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToKlineUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Advanced Trade ticker -> socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Advanced Trade batched ticker -> socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToBatchedTickerUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Advanced Trade symbols/status -> socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToSymbolUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Advanced Trade level2 -> socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Advanced Trade user -> socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToUserUpdatesAsync(handler)
Advanced Trade futures balance -> socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToFuturesBalanceUpdatesAsync(handler)
Exchange heartbeat -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToHeartbeatUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Exchange status -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToExchangeInfoUpdatesAsync(handler)
Exchange ticker -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Exchange batched ticker -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToBatchedTickerUpdatesAsync(symbol, handler)
Exchange level2 -> socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(symbol, onSnapshot, onUpdate)
```

## Market Data Example

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net.Clients;
using Coinbase.Net.Enums;

var client = new CoinbaseRestClient();

var product = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH-USD");
if (!product.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(product.Error);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine($"{product.Data.Symbol}: {product.Data.LastPrice}");

var candles = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetKlinesAsync(
    "ETH-USD",
    KlineInterval.OneMinute,
    limit: 10);

if (!candles.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(candles.Error);
    return;
}

foreach (var candle in candles.Data)
    Console.WriteLine($"{candle.OpenTime:u} close={candle.ClosePrice}");
```

## Account Balance Example

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net;
using Coinbase.Net.Clients;

var client = new CoinbaseRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials("API_KEY_NAME", "EC_PRIVATE_KEY");
});

var accounts = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.Account.GetAccountsAsync(limit: 100);
if (!accounts.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(accounts.Error);
    return;
}

foreach (var account in accounts.Data.Accounts)
    Console.WriteLine($"{account.Asset}: available={account.AvailableBalance.Value}, hold={account.HoldBalance.Value}");
```

## Spot Order Example

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net;
using Coinbase.Net.Clients;
using Coinbase.Net.Enums;

var client = new CoinbaseRestClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials("API_KEY_NAME", "EC_PRIVATE_KEY");
});

var order = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    symbol: "ETH-USD",
    side: OrderSide.Buy,
    orderType: NewOrderType.Limit,
    quantity: 0.01m,
    price: 2000m);

if (!order.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Error);
    return;
}

if (!order.Data.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Data.ErrorResponse.Message);
    return;
}

Console.WriteLine(order.Data.SuccessResponse.OrderId);
```

For market buy orders that specify quote currency amount, use `quoteQuantity`:

```csharp
var order = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.PlaceOrderAsync(
    "ETH-USD",
    OrderSide.Buy,
    NewOrderType.Market,
    quoteQuantity: 100m);
```

## Futures And Perpetual Example

```csharp
var futuresPositions = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.GetFuturesPositionsAsync();
if (!futuresPositions.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(futuresPositions.Error);
    return;
}

var perpetualPositions = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.Trading.GetPerpetualPositionsAsync("PORTFOLIO_ID");
if (!perpetualPositions.Success)
    Console.WriteLine(perpetualPositions.Error);
```

Use product ids returned by `GetSymbolsAsync(SymbolType.Futures)` for futures/perpetual products. Do not invent contract ids.

## WebSocket Subscription Example

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net.Clients;

var socketClient = new CoinbaseSocketClient();

var tickerSub = await socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToTickerUpdatesAsync(
    "ETH-USD",
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.LastPrice));

if (!tickerSub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(tickerSub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(tickerSub.Data);
```

Multiple subscriptions should all be checked before `.Data` is used:

```csharp
var bookSub = await socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(
    "ETH-USD",
    update => Console.WriteLine(update.Data.Bids.Length));

if (!bookSub.Success)
{
    await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(tickerSub.Data);
    Console.WriteLine(bookSub.Error);
    return;
}
```

## Private WebSocket Example

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net;
using Coinbase.Net.Clients;

var socketClient = new CoinbaseSocketClient(options =>
{
    options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials("API_KEY_NAME", "EC_PRIVATE_KEY");
});

var userSub = await socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SubscribeToUserUpdatesAsync(
    update =>
    {
        foreach (var order in update.Data.Orders)
            Console.WriteLine($"{order.Symbol}: {order.Status}");
    });

if (!userSub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(userSub.Error);
    return;
}

Console.ReadLine();

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(userSub.Data);
```

## Exchange API WebSocket Example

```csharp
var socketClient = new CoinbaseSocketClient();

var level2Sub = await socketClient.ExchangeApi.SubscribeToOrderBookUpdatesAsync(
    "ETH-USD",
    snapshot => Console.WriteLine($"Snapshot bids: {snapshot.Data.Bids.Length}"),
    update => Console.WriteLine($"Changes: {update.Data.Change.Length}"));

if (!level2Sub.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(level2Sub.Error);
    return;
}

await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(level2Sub.Data);
```

## SharedApis Multi-Exchange Pattern

Use CryptoExchange.Net.SharedApis when the user asks for code that can work against Coinbase and other exchanges.

```csharp
using Coinbase.Net.Clients;

var shared = new CoinbaseRestClient().AdvancedTradeApi.SharedClient;

Console.WriteLine(shared.Exchange);
Console.WriteLine(string.Join(", ", shared.SupportedTradingModes));

var info = shared.Discover();
var supported = info.Features.Where(x => x.Supported).Select(x => x.EndpointName);
Console.WriteLine($"{info.Exchange} {info.TypeName}: {string.Join(", ", supported)}");
```

Native Coinbase endpoints remain better when the user needs Coinbase-specific fields or parameters:

```text
Advanced Trade order configuration
CoinbaseOrderResult.Success/ErrorResponse
Portfolios
Payment methods
Coinbase App deposits/withdrawals/transactions
Futures/perpetual account details
Exchange API WebSocket level2 callbacks
```

Shared WebSocket client:

```csharp
var socketClient = new CoinbaseSocketClient();
var sharedSocket = socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi.SharedClient;
```

For shared socket interface variables, keep the concrete `CoinbaseSocketClient` so you can call:

```csharp
await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(subscription.Data);
```

## Symbol And Quantity Handling

Use product metadata instead of hard-coded precision:

```csharp
var product = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH-USD");
if (!product.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(product.Error);
    return;
}

var baseIncrement = product.Data.BaseIncrement;
var quoteIncrement = product.Data.QuoteIncrement;
```

Do not manually round order quantities and prices using string operations. Prefer symbol metadata returned by Coinbase or CryptoExchange.Net helpers when available.

Common order validation problems:

```text
Invalid product id format
Quantity below minimum
Quantity increment does not match base increment
Price increment does not match quote increment
Insufficient balance
Order type requires quoteQuantity instead of quantity
Private key formatting/newline issue
Account permissions missing
```

## Error Handling Notes

Common categories of Coinbase failures:

```text
Rate limiting or temporary network failure: transient; back off and retry if appropriate.
Timestamp/JWT signing issue: verify AutoTimestamp and local clock.
Credential issue: key name, ECDSA private key, permissions, or framework support; do not retry blindly.
Invalid product id: use dash-separated Coinbase product ids.
Order validation failure: fix quantity, price, increments, order type, permissions, or balance.
Exchange-level order rejection: HttpResult succeeds, but CoinbaseOrderResult.Success is false.
Unknown order: may be expected for filled, canceled, or wrong id queries.
```

Coinbase.Net result objects contain `Error.Code` and `Error.Message` when available:

```csharp
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(result.Error?.Code);
    Console.WriteLine(result.Error?.Message);
    return;
}
```

## AI-Friendly Examples

Use these examples when generating code:

```text
Examples/ai-friendly/01-advanced-trade-market-and-account.cs
  Advanced Trade public product, candles, order book, Exchange API products, and credential-gated accounts.

Examples/ai-friendly/02-advanced-trade-trading.cs
  Dry-run safe order workflow, open orders, fills, order placement, and cancellation.

Examples/ai-friendly/03-websocket.cs
  Advanced Trade ticker/order book streams, optional authenticated user stream, and teardown.

Examples/ai-friendly/04-shared-client.cs
  CryptoExchange.Net shared REST/socket client access, capability discovery, and native Coinbase endpoints.

Examples/ai-friendly/05-error-handling.cs
  HttpResult and WebSocketResult helpers, CoinbaseOrderResult handling, and subscription teardown.
```

These files are intended to be copied into a console `Program.cs` after `dotnet add package Coinbase.Net`. Private and trading examples read credentials from environment variables and avoid live order placement unless explicitly enabled.

## Anti-Hallucination Checklist

Before finalizing generated code:

- Does it compile with the current package?
- Does it include `using Coinbase.Net;` when using credentials or environments?
- Does every REST/socket result check `.Success` before `.Data`?
- Does order placement check `order.Data.Success`?
- Does it use `restClient.AdvancedTradeApi` or `restClient.ExchangeApi` correctly?
- Does it use `socketClient.AdvancedTradeApi` or `socketClient.ExchangeApi` correctly?
- Does it avoid raw Coinbase URLs and manual signing?
- Does it avoid nonexistent Binance/Bybit-style roots?
- Does it avoid HMAC/passphrase examples?
- Does it avoid `.Result` and `.Wait()`?
- Are WebSocket subscriptions unsubscribed on shutdown?
- For shared socket interfaces, does it keep the concrete socket client for `UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data)`?
- Does it use Coinbase dash-separated product ids?
- For order quantities/prices, does it use product metadata or a proper helper instead of ad hoc rounding?
- Does private endpoint code target a framework that supports the signing requirements?

## Source Of Truth For Method Names

When unsure, inspect these files instead of guessing:

```text
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ICoinbaseRestClient.cs
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ICoinbaseSocketClient.cs
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/AdvancedTradeApi/ICoinbaseRestClientAdvancedTradeApi.cs
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/AdvancedTradeApi/ICoinbaseRestClientAdvancedTradeApiExchangeData.cs
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/AdvancedTradeApi/ICoinbaseRestClientAdvancedTradeApiAccount.cs
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/AdvancedTradeApi/ICoinbaseRestClientAdvancedTradeApiTrading.cs
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/AdvancedTradeApi/ICoinbaseSocketClientAdvancedTradeApi.cs
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/ICoinbaseRestClientExchangeApi.cs
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/ICoinbaseRestClientExchangeApiExchangeData.cs
Coinbase.Net/Interfaces/Clients/ExchangeApi/ICoinbaseSocketClientExchangeApi.cs
Coinbase.Net/Enums/*.cs
```

Prefer the interface files over memory when generating endpoint names, parameters, callback names, and return types.

## Common Wrong Patterns To Avoid

Wrong:

```csharp
var client = new CoinbaseClient();
```

Use:

```csharp
var client = new CoinbaseRestClient();
```

Wrong:

```csharp
var ticker = await client.SpotApi.ExchangeData.GetTickerAsync("ETHUSDT");
```

Use:

```csharp
var ticker = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH-USD");
```

Wrong:

```csharp
options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials("key", "secret", "passphrase");
```

Use:

```csharp
options.ApiCredentials = new CoinbaseCredentials("key-name", "ec-private-key");
```

Wrong:

```csharp
var result = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH-USD");
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.LastPrice);
```

Use:

```csharp
var result = await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetSymbolAsync("ETH-USD");
if (!result.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(result.Error);
    return;
}
Console.WriteLine(result.Data.LastPrice);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
if (order.Success)
    Console.WriteLine(order.Data.SuccessResponse.OrderId);
```

Use:

```csharp
if (!order.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Error);
    return;
}
if (!order.Data.Success)
{
    Console.WriteLine(order.Data.ErrorResponse.Message);
    return;
}
Console.WriteLine(order.Data.SuccessResponse.OrderId);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
await sharedTickerSocket.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Use:

```csharp
await socketClient.UnsubscribeAsync(sub.Data);
```

Wrong:

```csharp
await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("ETHUSDT");
```

Use:

```csharp
await client.AdvancedTradeApi.ExchangeData.GetOrderBookAsync("ETH-USD");
```
